stem: A CPU advertised at 3.6 GHz has a higher clock speed than a CPU advertised at 2.0 GHz.
model_answer: Clock speed is the number of fetch–decode–execute cycles a CPU can perform per second (measured in Hz). A higher clock speed means more instructions can be completed per second, so (assuming other factors are equal) the computer will run faster.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain how a higher clock speed affects CPU performance.

mark_points.text: clock speed is the number of fetch–decode–execute cycles per second
a higher clock speed means more instructions can be processed each second, so the CPU runs faster
